modification_events.confidence_note: DM to Devon (S035) is originating message; S038 is the same update restated in channel ~2.5h later — not a separate event per decision #7.
E006 announces the post-call plan change and the sub-task 'update the proposal doc tonight' — per decision #1, doc update rolls up here as status_update on c_002, not as a new commitment.

Jordan's sign-off completes c_002. L006 (Alex on Linear) and S058 (Alex in channel) are cross-tool restatements — not separate events per decision #7.

L009 raised the possibility ('might need to push'); L010 was Maya's permission; L011 is Sam's firm ETA update. Anchored to L011 as the moment the slip became a commitment, not L009.
Jordan delivered notes Tuesday afternoon, two days ahead of the Thursday due date.

Same Linear comment as m_002 — L004 references both c_001 and c_005 ('Schema diff lands today, pairing with Sam in the AM').

non_commitments.reason: Soft offer without specific task — 'I can help wherever needed.' Same modal as S003 but no concrete deliverable. Closest call in the data.
Adversarial 'I'll' that's idiomatic skepticism, not a promise — 'I'll believe it when I see it.' Planted to test that the pipeline doesn't pattern-match on 'I'll'.
Conditional offer — 'happy to help if you need a frontend perspective on the rollout.' No task accepted unless invited.
Hedged with an explicit escape clause — 'I think I'll probably get to it if nothing else comes up.' Per decision #1, escape clauses disqualify.

Conditional transfer offer — 'if Sam wants me to take the dry-run back I can.' Declined in S042. Recorded as m_009 (transfer_declined) against c_003, but the message itself does not mint a new commitment.
Conditional offer — 'if you need someone to QA the FE after the migration, ping me.' Mirrors S010 from the same speaker.
Permission-granting, not commitment — 'Friday AM is fine if it has to slip.' Maya is approving Sam's request, not promising anything herself.
